event_title.x: GET PHIT Bootcamp - Texas A&M International University (TAMIU) July 2024
resource_improvement_feedback: Actually going in depth in how to use excel.
course_change_suggestions: I actually really hope this gets read. I think that in order to accomplish the GET aspect to GET-PHIT - we need to focus more on actually helping the students develop the skills regarding interpreting and analyzing data using tools like excel and IBM SPSS. It would have been better that more time was dedicated to making sure we had these skills and THEN asking groups to develop an idea using the data of what they would like to develop as a project. This way students with good ideas and continued interest in learning and presenting have until September to get together with their mentors throughout the two month span and develop their projects into something with potential to make change. Two weeks does not feel enough to truly delve into literature and find relevant sources. This course has the potential to change lives and introduce people into a growing field - the time is what is missing, but I still feel that I did NOT gain equity in training and I am saddened by this as I was truly interested and STILL AM interested in the field of public health. I, as an underprivileged and impoverished first gen jewish queer woman of colour, cannot say I walked away with skills that put me on par with people who are socioeconomically better off than me - and who actually dominate the field such as white folk, men, and cishet well off people.
disagreement_explanation_1: I feel that there was not enough time dedicated to learning how to read and analyze the data sets we were looking at. I would have really liked to have learned how to use the tool pack to analyze the data more in depth.
bootcamp_general_feedback: Id appreciate it a lot if feedback like mine was listened to. This program was incredible and continues to have a lot of potential. Hoping to attend next year if possible.
key_learning_supports: The class days that discussed how different fields have a place in public health and the different fields we could enter were exciting and helpful to learn about. Dr.Fenton was also very approachable and kind and open to provide insight and personal advise for those wanting to enter the field.
future_interest_topics_1: Health Care Management and Technology Adoption.
future_interest_topics_2: Surveillance and how to develop better EHR recording and tracking to reduce disparities in accumulated data regarding BIPOC and LGBTQ people.
independence_assistance_level: No; I would require a significant amount of assistance.

event_title.x: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas at Arlington (UTA) June 2025
resource_improvement_feedback: Implement activities during the lecture week. It wasnt easy to sit through hours of lecture at a time. Additionally, the material was very public health-focused rather than data and informatics-focused. When the program was advertised to me, it gave the impression that it would be very tech-integrated, but we were only instructed on how to use Excel. Some lectures on Tableau and ArcGIS, as well as hands-on experience with code, would have been great skills to learn.
course_change_suggestions: Including more lectures about coding and hands-on experience in using software to facilitate data analysis. We only really got a lecture covering Excel during our first week. More time spent in ArcGIS or even Tableau would have been better, since most of us already use Excel.
bootcamp_general_feedback: Overall, coming from a Public health student, the information taught at the bootcamp was vital in understanding PHI, but was more skewed to understanding public health. Lots of the information was review for me. I believe it needs to be equivalent to the amount of tech information taught. So, more tech and data-driven lectures as opposed to mostly public health information. Additionally, including activities in each lesson, since there was such a time constraint, maybe cutting down on the lecture materials to leave enough room for activity work. Lots of people learn best when applying the info, which aids students in retaining information they have just learned. I was hoping to get hands-on experience in coding or other software besides Excel, but given the time constraint, I guess that part couldnt able to be taught. I really was appreciative of the internship opportunities post bootcamp, I believe this helps equip students in getting their foot in the door post graduation!
key_learning_supports: During our project week, our instructors were readily available to us when needed. When structuring our presentation, they aided us in what parts were the most important to include. Additionally, they helped us look for data in places we didnt have access to. Which was extremely helpful.
future_interest_topics_1: Decision Modeling and Natural Language Processing.
independence_assistance_level: No; I would require a significant amount of assistance.

course_change_suggestions: I would recommend placing a stronger focus on hands-on learning with public health data systems and analytic tools that are actually used in the field. We all have differnt levels of understanding and learning, although it is easier to teach a student with more understanding than one without, because of time constraints. resource_improvement_feedback: The reason I picked disagree to the instructional materials question was for a couple of reasons. First, the presentation materials were generally not the most well explained and depending on the lecturer could create issues with understanding. Additionally, in a couple of the lectures the content was not well explained, especially to an audience who might be unfamiliar with the material, such as with simple linear regression. Though when there was a lecturer who was good an explaining and giving examples to the material, I feel this bootcamp was at its strongest.
course_change_suggestions: I would like the integration of current research on various topics covered in this course. Not even necessarily covering the research as a class, just being shown some of the reputable and important journals in fields related to public health informatics.
bootcamp_general_feedback: I believe that some of the lectures and materials for them could be better suited to the average undergraduate who may not be the most familiar with statistics or at the very least could be oriented to basic statistics that can be explained thoroughly in a couple of hours, such as T-tests or Chi-square, rather than something like regression. I found the explanations of regression to be lacking in clarity and practical applications to our projects and generally felt like the time could have been better spent. Overall though, this bootcamp was an amazing experience that I am very happy to have had the opportunity to attend. So thank you for going through the process of creating this program and bringing it to UTPB.
key_learning_supports: Dr. Ross was incredible at creating an engaged learning environment through mini-presentations and class discussions. I feel this helped going into the 2nd week as we already had to work together some with our group and knew some weaknesses and strengths.
independence_assistance_level: Yes; I am capable of performing analysis independently.

event_title.x: GET PHIT Bootcamp - University of Texas Permian Basin May 2024
resource_improvement_feedback: The provided datasets for the project were not exactly helpful, some pacing and PowerPoints for the course did not seem necessary. I believe the program would benefit from being more pointed/specific in its goals and direction. Learning about epidemiology for two hours was not useful for our end project.
course_change_suggestions: The group system needs to be reworked. There were issues among groups that could have been solved with more attentive or robust management, or if people were allowed to choose groups after the first or second day of the camp. Additionally, the speed of the course feels a bit uneasy. The first week seemed to fade away and not necessarily prepare us for research if we did not already understand what to do. We spent hours on figuring out thirty second introduction speeches - that is not necessary.
disagreement_explanation_1: The objectives and learning outcomes of this Bootcamp were relatively non-precise. I feel that too many topics were presented in a disorganized way that did not help me understand what the end learning goal was, that was something that I have gleaned myself. I genuinely do not feel that I have gained any new work skills from this Bootcamp, though this may be because I entered with a background in this already.
disagreement_explanation_2: Ethnicity and culture regions were heavily dependent on who is presenting these items. I think they were good, but must be more pointed to talk about racial/ethnicity bias.
bootcamp_general_feedback: I enjoyed it, but this could definitely be done better and there are ways that I and other students could be involved in planning to ensure that it is more efficient, successful, and productive in learning outcomes.
key_learning_supports: Instructors were open to questions and open to helping students understand. The course is definitely important for many fields to understand. Allowing for a multitude of backgrounds to participate encourages student engagement.
future_interest_topics_1: Decision Modeling and Natural Language Processing.
independence_assistance_level: No; I would require a little assistance.

instructor_comments_extra: I noticed that Dr. Cervantes edited the slides of his part of the presentation & even added a few of his own to further explain & help us understand certain concepts. I appreciated this greatly & was very refreshing to see after watching a previous lecturer just read straight off the slides.
resource_improvement_feedback: I feel that using other methods besides lecturing to teach us the information of the course would make this boot camp a lot better. To have us sitting in one room for 6+ hours listening to instructors just talk & reread things we can see on the slides doesn’t really instill information in anyone.
course_change_suggestions: I recommend presenting the criteria for the final project first before starting any of the lectures, that way we as students know which topics are going to be most relevant to us & the research topics we choose.
bootcamp_general_feedback: I really did not appreciate the fact that the dorm facilities were extremely dirty upon arrival, & that the a/c in the dorms was not working at ALL. As someone who’s been going to HT this is nothing new for me, but to have to explain to someone who doesn’t attend this university, that it’s always like that is actually quite embarrassing . It would just be better for the school over all to make sure their facilities are working before inviting anyone to come & stay. It’s a joke to have to have stayed there for two weeks with no a/c. I realize that those who were running the bootcamp have no say in whether the a/c works but still. Guess it’s just something to look forward to in the school year.
key_learning_supports: Like I mentioned before, Dr. Cervantes adding his own touch to the slides was really neat. Allowing our groups to work separately away from the other groups is another I liked about the project. For my team I know this helped us focus a lot more rather than being in the same room as everyone else & there being more distractions. Lastly, I enjoyed the portion of the camp where Dr.Ross was speaking to us about our future careers. She encouraged us to really look at the requirements needed to get into the schools & programs we wanted, & this was really an eye opener for me.
future_interest_topics_1: Health Care Management and Technology Adoption.
